designRead.businessType: General Contractor with HVAC positioning in supplied notes
availableFacts: Notes mention 817 Broadway, New York, NY 10003, USA
designRead.concept: A refined Manhattan HVAC field guide using editorial typography, thermal-line CSS art, local proof, and phone-first conversion instead of stock imagery.
hardConstraints: Do not edit app/page.tsx or app/globals.css during planning mode.
Do not invent licenses, guarantees, warranties, emergency service, same-day service, years in business, services not supported by HVAC context, or fake reviews.
Do not use unrelated stock images or hotlinked assets.
Preserve static Next.js and Vercel-friendly implementation.

dials.rationale: High visual specificity is needed because there are no photos or brand assets; motion stays restrained for a trust-first local service page.

colourScheme.usage: Warm editorial base with graphite contrast, copper CTAs, and cool blue HVAC accents. Avoid one-hue beige dominance and avoid SaaS gradients.

rules: Use Fraunces for H1, major section titles, pull statements, and large factual numerals.
Use Instrument Sans for body, navigation, CTAs, labels, tabs, and utility text.
Keep the desktop hero headline near 2-3 lines with a wide text container.
Do not use Arial, Helvetica, raw system-ui, or browser defaults as the visual identity.
Use clamp() for display type and spacing; do not scale fonts directly with viewport width.

customFonts.fallbackPolicy: Use safe fallback families only after the custom font variables; the intended identity must come from next/font.

assetPlan.photos: No supplied photos. Do not add stock imagery.
assetPlan.logo: No supplied logo. Create a simple text lockup or monogram in markup/CSS only.
visualSystem: Hero thermal map panel with CSS building grid, airflow curves, measurement ticks, and warm/cool split.
Manhattan grid-inspired proof panel built with CSS lines, not an external map.
Duct/vent linework dividers and small temperature chips.
High-contrast final CTA with graphite, copper, and cool line accents.
assetPlan.futurePhotoRule: If business.photos or business.heroImage is later supplied, use those real assets in controlled photo ribbons or panel crops and keep object-fit cover.

sections.purpose: Premium sticky navigation with brand lockup, anchor links, and call CTA.
sections.content: Brand name, Manhattan HVAC context, anchors to Approach, Services, Proof, Process, Contact, and Call now button.

sections.purpose: Art-directed first viewport that makes the page specific to Manhattan HVAC.
sections.content: Large editorial headline, short deck, call CTA, website CTA, right-side thermal contact panel with phone, service area, rating, and review count.

sections.purpose: Quick factual confidence without fake badges.
sections.content: Manhattan HVAC, 4.9 rating, 42 Google reviews, Broadway listing from notes.

sections.purpose: Explain the practical, room-first approach without generic about-us copy.
sections.content: Editorial two-column section about describing the room, system, symptoms, building access, and caller priorities.

sections.purpose: Present broad HVAC help areas without pretending a supplied service catalog exists.
sections.content: Selector panels for Cooling concerns, Heating concerns, Comfort diagnostics, and Manhattan property coordination.

sections.purpose: Help comparison shoppers know when and how to call.
sections.content: Asymmetric bento: symptoms to mention, building details to know, website verification, and unsupported-claim transparency.

sections.purpose: Use supplied proof and location context as a designed module.
sections.content: 4.9 rating, 42 Google reviews, Manhattan service area, Broadway listing from notes, website link.
Call with issue and location; describe system, symptoms, and access; discuss next appropriate service step.

sections.purpose: Turn limited data into transparent decision support rather than fake claims.
sections.content: Clear contact paths, factual proof only, Manhattan-specific call prep, no unsupported promises.
Derived FAQs about what to mention when calling, whether email is listed, website use, and service area.
Call (929) 298-9750, visit rileys-hvac.com, Manhattan context, rating/review proof.

sections.purpose: Close with navigation, contact paths, and data transparency.
sections.content: Business name, phone, website, anchors, service area, note that page uses supplied business data.

interactions.anchors: Header links scroll to sections and use focus-visible states.
interactions.serviceSelector: Use accessible CSS radio tabs or minimal client-state buttons if necessary; default to Cooling concerns.
interactions.accordion: Use native details/summary for FAQ disclosures.
interactions.mobileStickyCta: Fixed bottom call bar on small viewports when phone exists.
interactions.hoverFocus: Buttons and panels receive subtle color, border, and transform changes; no distracting animation.
interactions.reducedMotion: Disable transitions and smooth scrolling under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce.

decisionGuide.label: Have these details ready, then call

ctaMap.excluded: No email CTA because no email is supplied.

layout: Single-column sections with redesigned spacing, not squeezed desktop grids.
Hero headline remains readable in 3-5 mobile lines with clamp() and max-width controls.
Thermal map panel moves below hero text with stable aspect ratio.
Credibility strip wraps into two rows or horizontal scroller with no clipped text.
Service tabs stack into full-width segmented controls.
Bento and proof grids collapse to one column.
Sticky process layout becomes standard timeline.
Final CTA includes bottom padding so the sticky call bar does not cover content.
technical: Set overflow-x: hidden on html/body/page shell.
Use minmax(0, 1fr) in grids to avoid overflow.
Use focus-visible states large enough for touch navigation.
Use a bottom safe-area inset for mobile sticky CTA.

tasks: Update app/layout.tsx with metadata and next/font/google variables.
Create CSS tokens, reset, typography, layout primitives, focus states, and reduced-motion rules.
Rewrite app/page.tsx from a blank canvas using the planned component order.
Build all sections with factual data and CSS-art visual assets.
Implement service selector, native FAQ details, anchor navigation, and mobile sticky call bar.
Keep all interactions accessible and static-build safe.
Check hierarchy, section rhythm, button contrast, and mobile collapse.
Remove generic copy, repeated-card dominance, placeholder labels, and unsupported claims.
Inspect desktop and mobile if browser tooling is available.
Check no stock assets, no fake claims, no missing phone CTA, and no horizontal overflow.

qaChecklist: At least 9 meaningful sections plus footer are present.
Custom fonts are loaded through next/font/google.
Hero is art-directed and specific to Manhattan HVAC.
Phone and website CTAs use real href values.
No stock images or external hotlinked images are used.
No fake licenses, warranties, emergency availability, same-day service, guarantees, testimonials, or years in business are claimed.
Rating and review count are only shown as supplied notes facts.
Service language remains broad and HVAC-grounded because services array is empty.
Navigation anchors work and focus states are visible.
Mobile sticky call bar works and does not obscure content.
No horizontal overflow on common mobile widths.
Final CTA and footer are complete.
The page could not pass as a generic local-business template by only changing the business name.
